semantic.role: Primary project documentation for the ArchSpine protocol and toolchain, defining its purpose, architecture, conventions, and operational discipline.
responsibilities: Describing ArchSpine as an open-source architectural governance and semantic layer with a physical .spine/ control plane inside Git repositories
Listing tech stack (TypeScript, Node.js, Vitest, ESLint, Prettier, VitePress, better-sqlite3, @ast-grep/napi, MCP SDK) and common development commands
Documenting code conventions (ES modules, strict TypeScript, 2-space indent, naming, commit message format with optional scopes)
Explaining the architecture layers (cli, core, engines, services, infra, tasks, types, utils, ast) and their responsibilities
Defining the Git workflow, branching model, and PR template requirements

Stating key constraints: bilingual documentation (English + Simplified Chinese), .spine/ dogfooding protocol, prohibition on direct edits to generated .spine/ directories, and discipline for pipeline commits
Providing detailed pipeline and commit discipline rules for maintaining .spine/ as a distribution artifact
outOfScope: Detailed API reference or function-level documentation
End-user guide or non-developer usage instructions
Historical design, planning, or archive documents (separate docs/ directories)
Internal design rationale beyond what is stated in the constraints

English.purpose: This document introduces ArchSpine, an open-source protocol and toolchain that embeds a physical .spine/ control plane inside Git repositories. It explains the project's high-level vision—making codebases queryable, governable, and auditable for AI-assisted engineering—and provides all necessary conventions, commands, and discipline guidelines for contributors and maintainers.
English.context_and_audience: The primary audience is developers and maintainers working on ArchSpine itself, as well as AI agents that interact with the repository. It assumes familiarity with TypeScript, Node.js, and modern Git workflows. The document is the entry point for understanding the project's structure, development practices, and the strict commit discipline required to keep the .spine/ artifact consistent.
key_takeaways: ArchSpine builds a .spine/ control plane in every Git repo to make codebases AI-queryable and governable.
The tech stack is strictly TypeScript 5 ESM, Node.js >=20, with specific test, lint, and documentation tooling.
Code conventions enforce ES module imports with .js suffixes, strict types, 2-space indent, and Conventional Commits with optional scopes.
Architecture is layered: cli, core, engines, services, infra, tasks, types, utils, ast—each with distinct responsibilities.
Key constraints include bilingual docs (EN + Simplified Chinese), dogfooding .spine/ protocol, never editing generated artifacts directly, and using spine sync instead.
The commit discipline mandates separating source changes from .spine/ sync commits, with agents required to run the formal sync command and verify staged files.

Simplified Chinese.purpose: 本文档介绍 ArchSpine，一个开源协议与工具链，它在 Git 仓库内部建立一个物理的 `.spine/` 控制平面。文档阐述了项目的高层愿景——让代码库对 AI 辅助工程具有可查询、可治理和可审计的能力——并为贡献者和维护者提供了所有必要的约定、命令和纪律指南。
Simplified Chinese.context_and_audience: 主要受众是 ArchSpine 自身的开发者与维护者，以及与该仓库交互的 AI 代理。假设读者熟悉 TypeScript、Node.js 和现代 Git 工作流。本文档是理解项目结构、开发实践以及维护 `.spine/` 制品一致性所需的严格提交纪律的入口。
key_takeaways: ArchSpine 在每个 Git 仓库中构建 `.spine/` 控制平面，使代码库对 AI 可查询、可治理。
技术栈严格限定为 TypeScript 5 ESM、Node.js >=20，使用特定的测试、代码检查和文档工具。
代码约定要求显式的 `.js` 导入后缀、严格类型、2空格缩进，并使用带可选范围的约定式提交。
关键约束包括双语文档（英文 + 简体中文）、对 `.spine/` 协议的自举使用、绝不直接编辑生成的制品，而是使用 `spine sync` 命令。
提交纪律要求将源码变更与 `.spine/` 同步分开提交，AI 代理必须运行正式的 sync 命令并检查暂存文件分类。
